stories.title: Hamilton Grabs Sprint Pole at Silverstone — Ferrari's Home Dream Begins

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
HAMILTON ON SPRINT POLE AT SILVERSTONE. 🔴

In a Ferrari. At his home race. 0.011 seconds ahead of championship leader Antonelli.

He called it an "amazing surprise." The crowd will call it electric.

Sprint race today at noon BST. #BritishGP #F1

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Sprint qualifying top 3 at Silverstone:

1. Hamilton (Ferrari) — 1:28.376
2. Antonelli (Mercedes) — +0.011s
3. Verstappen (Red Bull) — +0.343s

Hamilton was fastest in practice AND all three sprint qualifying segments.

Home hero on form. 🔴🇬🇧 #BritishGP #F1

facebook.long_form:
🔴 HAMILTON ON POLE AT SILVERSTONE — IN A FERRARI

Lewis Hamilton made a statement at his home circuit on Friday. The seven-time world champion took sprint pole position at Silverstone by the narrowest margin of the season — just 0.011 seconds ahead of championship leader Kimi Antonelli.

Hamilton's fastest lap of 1:28.376 came in a Ferrari. At the track where he grew up watching Formula 1. At the circuit he has won at more than any other driver in history. And he called it an "amazing surprise."

The sprint qualifying top three:
1. Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari) — 1:28.376
2. Kimi Antonelli (Mercedes) — +0.011s
3. Max Verstappen (Red Bull) — +0.343s

Hamilton was the quickest driver in practice and then led all three segments of sprint qualifying. George Russell, the Mercedes driver who finished second in the championship last time out, had a difficult session and qualified fifth.

The sprint race runs Saturday at noon BST. 100,000 British fans will be inside Silverstone. They will be making noise for one man in red.
facebook.image_caption: Hamilton. Silverstone. Sprint pole. In a Ferrari. This is appointment viewing. 🔴🇬🇧 #BritishGP #F1

article.headline: Hamilton Takes Sprint Pole at Silverstone in Ferrari — "An Amazing Surprise" at His Home Race

stories.title: Sprint Race Day — Hamilton vs. Antonelli at Noon BST

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
TODAY at Silverstone:

🏁 Sprint Race — noon BST (7 AM ET)
🏁 GP Qualifying — 4 PM BST (11 AM ET)

Hamilton (Ferrari) vs. Antonelli (championship leader) from the front row.

100,000 fans. Zero rain forecast. Let's go. 🏎️ #BritishGP #F1

facebook.long_form:
🏁 SPRINT RACE DAY AT SILVERSTONE — HAMILTON VS. ANTONELLI

It is July 4, 2026, and the most anticipated sprint race of the Formula 1 season is about to begin.

Lewis Hamilton starts from pole position in his Ferrari at Silverstone — his home circuit, where he has won nine times in his career. Kimi Antonelli, the 21-year-old Mercedes driver who leads the 2026 World Championship by 40 points, starts second. Max Verstappen is third for Red Bull.

The sprint race runs over a short distance — 17 laps compared to the 52-lap Sunday race — and awards points to the top 8 finishers. For Antonelli, even a sprint podium extends his championship lead. For Hamilton, winning on home soil in Ferrari red would be one of the great moments of his legendary career.

Today's schedule at Silverstone:
🏎️ Sprint Race — noon BST (7 AM ET)
🏎️ GP Qualifying — 4 PM BST (11 AM ET)

Sunday: British Grand Prix main race — 3 PM BST (10 AM ET)

Weather is perfect. No rain. Mid-20s Celsius. The conditions are set for an incredible day of racing.
facebook.image_caption: Sprint race day at Silverstone. Hamilton from pole. Antonelli hunting points. The British GP weekend is in full swing. 🏁 #BritishGP #F1

article.headline: British GP Sprint Race Day: Hamilton vs. Antonelli — The Home Hero Meets the Championship Leader

stories.title: Cadillac's Stars-and-Stripes Livery at F1's Birthplace on July 4

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Happy July 4th from Silverstone 🎆🇺🇸

Cadillac is running a stars-and-stripes livery at F1's birthplace on America's 250th birthday.

Silverstone hosted the very first F1 World Championship race in 1950.

America's birthday meets the sport's birthplace. #F1 #BritishGP

facebook.long_form:
🇺🇸🎆 CADILLAC HONORS AMERICA'S 250th AT THE BIRTHPLACE OF F1

Happy Independence Day from Silverstone.

Today, July 4, 2026, marks the 250th anniversary of the United States of America — and Cadillac, the American Formula 1 team, is celebrating in style. The team is running a special stars-and-stripes livery at the British Grand Prix, bringing the red, white, and blue to the track that started it all.

Silverstone hosted the very first Formula 1 World Championship race on May 13, 1950. This circuit, in the heart of England, is where the sport's history was born. And today, America's newest F1 team is honoring its home country's birthday at that exact venue.

It has not been an easy debut season for Cadillac. After 8 races in 2026, the team has yet to score a championship point. The machinery is still catching up to the field's front-runners. But on July 4th, the cars look fantastic — draped in the colors of Independence.

Seventy-six years of Formula 1 history meeting 250 years of American history. There is something poetic about that.
facebook.image_caption: Cadillac running stars-and-stripes at F1's birthplace on America's 250th birthday. Only in Formula 1. 🇺🇸🎆 #F1 #BritishGP

article.headline: Stars, Stripes, and Silverstone: Cadillac Honors America's 250th at F1's Birthplace

stories.title: The Antonelli Phenomenon — Championship Leader at 21

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli after 8 races of the 2026 F1 season:

🏆 171 points
🏆 5 wins | 4 poles | 7 podiums
🏆 40 points ahead of Russell

He is 21 years old.

He qualified P2 at Silverstone — right behind Hamilton. The title is his to lose. #F1

facebook.long_form:
🏆 THE ANTONELLI PHENOMENON — DOMINATING F1 AT 21

Eight races into the 2026 Formula 1 World Championship season, and the conversation always comes back to the same name: Kimi Antonelli.

The 21-year-old Mercedes driver leads the drivers' championship with 171 points — 40 points clear of his teammate George Russell. In 8 races, Antonelli has 5 wins, 4 pole positions, and 7 podiums. He has finished off the podium just once.

For context on how extraordinary this is: drivers typically spend their first season learning the cars, learning the circuits, learning the politics of team radio and tire management and race strategy. Antonelli has skipped all of that and gone straight to dominating.

The 2026 championship standings after Race 8:
1. Kimi Antonelli (Mercedes) — 171 pts
2. George Russell (Mercedes) — 131 pts
3. Lando Norris (McLaren) — 79 pts
4. Charles Leclerc (Ferrari) — 79 pts
5. Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari) — further back

Saturday at Silverstone, Antonelli qualified second in sprint qualifying behind Hamilton by just 0.011 seconds. He is here. He is ready. And if he leaves Britain with his championship lead intact or extended, the 2026 title conversation is nearly over.
facebook.image_caption: Kimi Antonelli: 171 points, 5 wins, 7 podiums, 40-point championship lead — in his first full F1 season. The 2026 championship is his to lose. 🏆 #F1

article.headline: The Antonelli Phenomenon: F1's 21-Year-Old Championship Leader Is Rewriting What a Rookie Season Looks Like

stories.title: British GP Saturday — Sprint, Qualifying, and Sunday's Race Preview

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Your British GP Saturday guide:

⏱ Noon BST (7 AM ET) — Sprint Race
⏱ 4 PM BST (11 AM ET) — GP Qualifying

Then Sunday at 3 PM BST (10 AM ET) — the British Grand Prix.

First Silverstone sprint weekend since 2021. Don't miss it. 🏎️ #BritishGP #F1

facebook.long_form:
📋 BRITISH GP SATURDAY — YOUR COMPLETE GUIDE

Silverstone has one of the busiest Saturdays in recent F1 memory today. Here is everything happening:

🕛 Noon BST (7 AM ET) — SPRINT RACE (17 laps)
Hamilton from pole, Antonelli second, Verstappen third. Points go to the top 8. This is the shorter race format unique to sprint weekends.

🕓 4 PM BST (11 AM ET) — MAIN GP QUALIFYING
This session sets the grid for Sunday's 52-lap British Grand Prix. Sprint qualifying results do NOT determine Sunday's starting grid — a separate qualifying session runs this afternoon.

Then Sunday:
🕒 3 PM BST (10 AM ET) — BRITISH GRAND PRIX (Race 9/22)

Weather across all three days: zero rain, temperatures in the mid-20s Celsius. Silverstone conditions are as close to perfect as they get.

This is the first sprint weekend at Silverstone since 2021 — and given Hamilton's pole position and the championship drama between Antonelli and the rest of the field, it may be one of the best weekends of the 2026 F1 season.
facebook.image_caption: Sprint race at noon. GP qualifying at 4 PM. British Grand Prix Sunday at 3 PM. Silverstone's biggest Saturday in years. 🏎️ #BritishGP #F1

article.headline: British GP Saturday Guide: Sprint Race, Qualifying, and What to Watch at Silverstone
